jokes.text: Can we have the room? Fantastic speech, ma'am. The three Ss... strength, stability and bullshit.
jokes.setup: Ben congratulates the president on her speech and mentions the three S's
jokes.punchline: The third S is 'bullshit' instead of something noble
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of political speech conventions with perfect economy

jokes.text: I forgot to thank the voters for making our country look like a high school Spanish club.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the election results
jokes.punchline: Comparing America's political state to a niche high school club
jokes.explanation: Specific, unexpected comparison that captures both the absurdity and the scale of the situation

jokes.text: Didn't those founding fuckers ever hear of an odd number?
jokes.setup: The electoral college tie situation
jokes.punchline: Blaming the founding fathers for not anticipating basic math
jokes.explanation: Good character voice but relatively straightforward complaint about the system

jokes.text: Two great Greek contributions to society... democracy and getting fucked up the ass. I've tried both and they're way overrated, like jazz.
jokes.setup: Listing Greek contributions to civilization
jokes.punchline: Comparing democracy to anal sex and then adding jazz as a non-sequitur third thing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with unexpected third element (jazz) that perfectly captures Selina's crude intellectualism

jokes.text: My bowling coach used to say a tie was like kissing your sister. Well, this feels like my sister took a shit on my chest.
jokes.setup: Quoting a common sports saying about ties
jokes.punchline: Escalating the metaphor to something far more disgusting and specific
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of a familiar saying with shocking specificity

jokes.text: There is no world in which I am going to be vice president to that smug, Dick Van Dyke-looking motherfucker Tom James.
jokes.setup: Selina's frustration with the political situation
jokes.punchline: Comparing Tom James to Dick Van Dyke in an insult
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but the Dick Van Dyke comparison isn't particularly sharp

jokes.text: Oh, believe me, I know what she majored in. 65 grand a year to watch movies.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Catherine majored in film
jokes.punchline: Reducing film studies to just 'watching movies' with the high cost
jokes.explanation: Standard parental complaint about liberal arts education, competent but predictable

jokes.text: Okay, we don't have time to hear the story of your syndrome. What about your minor, dance? Why don't you make up a little kind of a dance about the election? You know, you can't decide should you dance or should you not dance?
jokes.setup: Catherine trying to explain her college struggles
jokes.punchline: Selina dismissing her problems and suggesting an interpretive dance
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Selina's dismissive nature but the execution is a bit wordy

jokes.text: You're gonna shatter the bulletproof glass in here.
jokes.setup: Catherine is whining
jokes.punchline: Her voice is so annoying it could break bulletproof glass
jokes.explanation: Nice specific escalation that plays with the presidential setting

jokes.text: Life gives you Yemen, you got to make Yemenade.
jokes.setup: Discussing the situation in Yemen
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'when life gives you lemons, make lemonade'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Selina's glib attitude toward international crises

jokes.text: The only thing Catherine ever finished was an entire ice cream cake.
jokes.setup: Dismissing Catherine's ability to finish projects
jokes.punchline: The only thing she completed was eating a whole cake
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Selina's cruel dismissal of her daughter with a specific image

jokes.text: Actually, four Christian missionaries burned to death. Oh, even better.
jokes.setup: Ben corrects that the Yemen situation involves deaths
jokes.punchline: Selina thinks dead missionaries make better cover
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark character moment showing Selina's callousness with perfect economy

jokes.text: This thing on the side of my face feels like a dog nipple.
jokes.setup: Selina describing her facial blemish
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a dog nipple
jokes.explanation: Weird, specific comparison that sets up the pimple running gag

jokes.text: It's hardly noticeable, ma'am, like our Hispanic voter turnout.
jokes.setup: Trying to reassure Selina about her pimple
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to their terrible Hispanic voter numbers
jokes.explanation: Sharp political callback that works as both reassurance and self-deprecation

jokes.text: I don't know what's getting their dicks harder, an electoral college tie or talking to a girl.
jokes.setup: Describing constitutional law experts working on the case
jokes.punchline: They're equally excited by the legal issue and female contact
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about nerdy legal experts with crude humor

jokes.text: It's a pimple that erupted during a very stressful time.
jokes.setup: Correcting that it's not a 'stress pimple'
jokes.punchline: Overly literal description that misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary pedantry that shows his character while being mildly amusing

jokes.text: For the stress pimple? Ben gets those on his butt. She should see a doctor.
jokes.setup: Discussion about treating the pimple
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning Ben's ass pimples
jokes.explanation: Kent's matter-of-fact delivery of gross personal information is characteristic

jokes.text: Actually, Wendy was keeping it a secret from me until today because I'm so bad with keeping secrets.
jokes.setup: Kent shares secret about adoption
jokes.punchline: Wife hid it because he can't keep secrets (while sharing this secret)
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-awareness while proving the point

jokes.text: You're a nincompoop. We are in the middle of a tied election. There is no time for some Chinese baby.
jokes.setup: Mike announcing he's adopting a baby
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete dismissal and calling him a nincompoop
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina selfishness with the old-fashioned insult 'nincompoop'

jokes.text: Cancel it and see if you can get your money back. Fortune cookie say you fuck up big-time, round eye.
jokes.setup: Mike's adoption announcement
jokes.punchline: Telling him to cancel and adding a racist fortune cookie joke
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's casual racism and cruelty but relies on stereotype

jokes.text: Too bad you're not counting your missteps. You'd be done by breakfast, right?
jokes.setup: Kent mentions counting his steps
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'missteps' vs steps with a time reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that ties into Selina's frustration with Kent

jokes.text: How many abortions does a pro-lifer have to pressure his mistress into before the people turn on him? Three.
jokes.setup: Discussing how Lafontaine won despite scandals
jokes.punchline: The specific number needed is three abortions
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire with a specific number that makes it feel researched and cynical

jokes.text: Yeah, drive sober. Take a shit without getting a hernia.
jokes.setup: Discussing what Selina can do that O'Brien can't
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and crude limitations of O'Brien
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation that establishes O'Brien's drinking problem and age

jokes.text: I don't think that we're symposium on race desperate yet.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to move up the race symposium
jokes.punchline: Implying there's a level of desperation where you resort to race discussions
jokes.explanation: Sharp political insight about how race becomes a political tool when convenient

jokes.text: There's only one person I'd be more surprised to see today in the Roosevelt Room and that's Teddy fucking Roosevelt.
jokes.setup: Ben's surprise at seeing Bill
jokes.punchline: Only a dead president would be more surprising
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that plays with the room's name and historical context

jokes.text: You're as welcome here as a swastika-shaped shit in a synagogue.
jokes.setup: Telling Bill how unwelcome he is
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and offensive comparison
jokes.explanation: Shocking specificity and imagery that emphasizes complete unwelcome status

jokes.text: Oh, they all look alike to me. What? Not Chinese people, but pictures of children.
jokes.setup: Selina makes what sounds like a racist comment
jokes.punchline: Clarifies she means baby photos, not Chinese people
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that plays with audience expectations and Selina's character

jokes.text: The agency says she's either 16 months or three years old.
jokes.setup: Discussing the age of the adoptive baby
jokes.punchline: Huge uncertainty about something basic as age
jokes.explanation: Absurd uncertainty about basic information highlights adoption bureaucracy

jokes.text: You ran one campaign for two weeks and had a nervous breakdown. That is medically inaccurate.
jokes.setup: Greg criticizing Dan's campaign experience
jokes.punchline: Dan only objects to the medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Dan's pedantic response while missing the bigger insult is perfect character work

jokes.text: Go out, win a race with a Jesus-loving homophobic homosexual or a racist billionaire.
jokes.setup: Advice for Dan to make his name
jokes.punchline: The contradictory and extreme candidate types needed to get noticed
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about the kind of extreme candidates that make careers

jokes.text: Any chance they fixed the Wi-Fi? I'll check.
jokes.setup: Told about Chinese hackers breaching NSA
jokes.punchline: Selina's only concern is if they improved the Wi-Fi
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's warped priorities with great economy

jokes.text: I am not quite seeing it, um, only because I'm... well, you are...
jokes.setup: Secret Service explaining Agent Palmiotti looks like her from behind
jokes.punchline: Selina's uncomfortable realization about what this implies
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment as Selina realizes the implication about her appearance

jokes.text: She smells completely different, so, you know...
jokes.setup: Asked to help explain why Agent Palmiotti doesn't look like Selina
jokes.punchline: Gary focuses on smell instead of the obvious visual difference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary response that avoids the obvious while being completely unhelpful

jokes.text: 'Nev-add-a.'
jokes.setup: Correcting pronunciation of Nevada
jokes.punchline: Kent's pedantic pronunciation correction during crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent pedantry about pronunciation at inappropriate moment

jokes.text: Nev-add-a. Oh, my God. I get to be president. I don't have to move.
jokes.setup: Learning about the Nevada recount possibility
jokes.punchline: Her excitement is about not having to move rather than serving the country
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's shallow motivations for wanting to be president

jokes.text: Two. Constitutional law and veterinary medicine, which was my fallback. But you were getting my coffee. Which is much harder 'cause you have so many different moods.
jokes.setup: Asked about having a doctorate while getting coffee
jokes.punchline: Making coffee is harder because of Selina's moods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard sweetness turning a compliment into insight about working for Selina

jokes.text: I actually did my doctorate in recount procedures in the West. Excellent work, Richard. Richard works for me.
jokes.setup: Richard reveals relevant expertise
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately claims credit for hiring him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina taking credit, Richard's oddly specific expertise

jokes.text: This is highly enriched weapons-grade bullshit.
jokes.setup: Jonah's outrage at reporting to Richard
jokes.punchline: Nuclear weapons terminology applied to workplace grievance
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to nuclear terminology, classic Jonah pomposity

jokes.text: She's Uncle Dursley and she's got the great wizard Harry Potter living under the staircase. Do you see that? But what happens in the Harry Potter books? He rises up and he kills all the muggles. I don't think that's what happens.
jokes.setup: Jonah comparing his situation to Harry Potter
jokes.punchline: Completely misremembering the plot of Harry Potter
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah stupidity with Richard's gentle correction showing the contrast

jokes.text: The Dow is dropping lower than my balls on a hot summer day.
jokes.setup: Describing the stock market crash
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his sagging anatomy
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective comparison that fits Ben's character

jokes.text: Zitzilla just stomped all over Wall Street.
jokes.setup: Blaming the market crash on the pimple coverage
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'Zitzilla' like Godzilla
jokes.explanation: Brilliant portmanteau that captures both the absurdity and the destructive power

jokes.text: He says, 'The market crash caused a blotch on the face of America.' Is that about my pimple?
jokes.setup: O'Brien's statement about the market crash
jokes.punchline: Selina thinks he's making a pimple reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect paranoia showing how the pimple has consumed Selina's thinking

jokes.text: You baked a fucking soufflé on my face.
jokes.setup: Blaming Gary for the pimple getting worse
jokes.punchline: Comparing the hot compress treatment to baking a soufflé
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific cooking metaphor that perfectly captures the situation

jokes.text: Did he just say zit? How come no one ever tries to assassinate a vice president?
jokes.setup: Someone greets her with 'howzit'
jokes.punchline: Mishearing it as 'zit' and wanting to be assassinated
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Selina's paranoia and death wish with perfect non-sequitur logic

jokes.text: Like an empty cop car they park on the side of the road to slow speeders. Those cars are empty?
jokes.setup: Comparing economy czar to deterrent measures
jokes.punchline: Richard's innocent surprise about police tactics
jokes.explanation: Sweet Richard innocence contrasted with Ben's cynical political thinking

jokes.text: I'm on it, ma'am, like brown on rice.
jokes.setup: Agreeing to handle the race symposium
jokes.punchline: Using a food metaphor that accidentally relates to race
jokes.explanation: Plays on common saying but in race context, though not particularly sharp

jokes.text: No, he's... God, he's deviating from the book of the things that he should do. You mean off the rails. Oh. Fine, he's off the rails.
jokes.setup: Selina struggling with the phrase 'off book'
jokes.punchline: Multiple corrections and eventual frustrated acceptance
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's confusion and stubbornness but execution is wordy

jokes.text: Look, regardless of the recount, this could mean big things for zucchini.
jokes.setup: Amy wrapping up a serious political conversation
jokes.punchline: Inexplicable reference to zucchini benefits
jokes.explanation: Completely random line that's funny for its absurdity and placement

jokes.text: You can't fire me, I quib. You know it's supposed to say quit. Well, I just fired you anyway. You can't quib, you rebard.
jokes.setup: Dan's autocorrected text message
jokes.punchline: Selina makes up 'rebard' to match his typo
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay escalation showing Selina's quick wit and cruelty

jokes.text: We are doubling down on O'Brien, so you're basically as useless to me as a 40-year-old woman.
jokes.setup: Firing Dan from TV show
jokes.punchline: Ageist and sexist comparison to worthless 40-year-old woman
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of TV industry ageism and sexism

jokes.text: It's called POTUS. Pimple of the United States. There are 220,000 followers as of five minutes ago. How many Twitter followers do I have? Approaching that.
jokes.setup: A Twitter account for the pimple
jokes.punchline: It has almost as many followers as the actual president
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the pimple storyline with perfect acronym and social media satire

jokes.text: Oh, czar she blows.
jokes.setup: Tom seeing Selina approaching to offer him the czar position
jokes.punchline: Play on 'thar she blows' whaling cry
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that anticipates the conversation and shows Tom's wit

jokes.text: Well, respectfully, Madam President, I hope you do because if the President of the United States can't tell the difference between Treasury Secretary and stock market patsy, that's pretty disturbing.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if he sees the difference between positions
jokes.punchline: Tom's cutting response about her competence
jokes.explanation: Sharp political wit disguised as respectful disagreement

jokes.text: I have to balance that against my need not to do it.
jokes.setup: Tom acknowledging the honor of the position
jokes.punchline: His greater need to avoid it entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect diplomatic phrasing that's completely honest about his intentions

jokes.text: I'm not picking up any respect. But you're getting the declining?
jokes.setup: Tom saying he's 'respectfully declining'
jokes.punchline: Selina breaking down the phrase to show it's not actually respectful
jokes.explanation: Clever parsing of diplomatic language showing Selina's sharp wit

jokes.text: If she does that to her own goddamn name, how the hell is she going to handle a potential recount?
jokes.setup: Learning Candi changed her name from Candice
jokes.punchline: Using name change as predictor of professional incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good logical extension from minor personal choice to professional competence

jokes.text: If she does that to her own goddamn name, how the hell is she going to handle a potential recount?
jokes.setup: Candice changed name to Candi
jokes.punchline: Name change indicates untrustworthiness for serious job
jokes.explanation: Sharp logic connecting personal choices to professional competence

jokes.text: All you're doing right now is making me hungry. So why don't you do something about that?
jokes.setup: Gary describing his food-based pimple remedy
jokes.punchline: Selina just wants him to get her the food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's self-centeredness

jokes.text: Hi, it's Candi with an I. Amy with a Y. Which is correct.
jokes.setup: Candi introducing herself with spelling clarification
jokes.punchline: Amy asserting her spelling is the correct one
jokes.explanation: Sharp put-down showing Amy's immediate dislike and superiority complex

jokes.text: You know Jonah. He works for me. In fact, you two used to date, but you never got over him.
jokes.setup: Richard introducing Jonah
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated romantic history
jokes.explanation: Sweet Richard trying to help Jonah with completely inappropriate lie

jokes.text: Cliff, my plate is just full of shit right now and I'd like for you to clean that.
jokes.setup: Jonah delegating tasks
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing his work as literally shit
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah inadvertently describing his work accurately

jokes.text: Well, I am enjoying a delicious sandwich made even more delicious by the fact that there's a homeless guy watching me eat it.
jokes.setup: Amy calling while Dan is eating
jokes.punchline: Dan's cruel enjoyment of contrast with homeless person
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Dan's sociopathic enjoyment of others' suffering

jokes.text: Right now you're about as toxic as a urinal cake in Chernobyl and I am offering you a job.
jokes.setup: Describing Dan's current reputation
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific toxic comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific comparison combining multiple levels of toxicity

jokes.text: 18,000 steps. I am Chinese baby ready.
jokes.setup: Mike celebrating his step count
jokes.punchline: Connecting fitness to adoption readiness
jokes.explanation: Sweet Mike logic connecting unrelated concepts

jokes.text: I LBJ'd you, okay? I'm the LBJ queen and you are Sargent 'Suck It' Shriver.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about being forced into the position
jokes.punchline: Selina comparing herself to LBJ and renaming Sargent Shriver
jokes.explanation: Excellent political reference with perfect name modification showing Selina's historical knowledge

jokes.text: Kind of like the national debt.
jokes.setup: Saying the pimple isn't getting smaller
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the ever-growing national debt
jokes.explanation: Decent political comparison but feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: Kind of like the national debt.
jokes.setup: Pimple not getting smaller
jokes.punchline: Comparison to ever-growing national debt
jokes.explanation: Quick political comparison, good economy

jokes.text: And this thing up my ass has got to get removed, too.
jokes.setup: Doctor asking what he can help with
jokes.punchline: Selina referring to Tom James as something in her ass
jokes.explanation: Good crude metaphor for Tom James as an irritant

jokes.text: Then I declare a state of go fuck yourself.
jokes.setup: Asked about declaring emergency for Idaho mudslides
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'state of emergency' with crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect presidential language subversion with great economy

jokes.text: I don't mean this to sound insensitive, but don't you think that mudslides are the funniest kind of natural disasters? I've always been partial to sinkholes. You know, ah! Those are hilarious. Nature's trapdoor.
jokes.setup: Discussing natural disasters
jokes.punchline: Ranking them by humor level and Kent's 'nature's trapdoor'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about disasters with Kent's perfect 'nature's trapdoor' addition

jokes.text: What the fuck is going on here? Why are they all white? Looks like the NHL All-Star Weekend.
jokes.setup: Seeing the race symposium participants
jokes.punchline: Comparing the lack of diversity to hockey
jokes.explanation: Sharp sports comparison that accurately captures the diversity problem

jokes.text: We need to round up some blacks and fast. We need to round up... Don't say it over the radio, asshole.
jokes.setup: Realizing they need diversity quickly
jokes.punchline: The terrible optics of the phrasing over radio
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment about poor word choice in sensitive situation

jokes.text: Faces from the savannahs of Africa to the cities and towns of England. Faces from... from Holland and even perhaps Norway.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to describe diversity at the symposium
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate listing of only white countries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of panic as Selina realizes her mistake and makes it worse

jokes.text: In your fucking face, Sue.
jokes.setup: Sue arriving just as Selina collapses
jokes.punchline: Selina's vindictive satisfaction at perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's petty vindictiveness even while collapsing
